care.tips: Native to: BLZ, STH OO, AGW SJ, BZC GO, AGE FO
Native to: BLZ, BZC GO, AGW SJ, BZE PB, AGE FO

description: greenish, pods (to 7” long) which mature in late summer and persist into fall

care.light_guide: NC State: Dappled Sunlight (Shade through upper canopy all day)
common_problems: There are no major pests of Cross-vine; however, the vine itself may become a problem. It spreads both by seed as well as by root suckers. To prevent the vine from spreading to areas where it is not wanted, pull up the root suckers as they are spotted.
common_pests: There are no major pests of Cross-vine; however, the vine itself may become a problem.

description: long, bean-like seed capsules (3-5” long) which split open when ripe releasing numerous 2-winged seeds for dispersal by the wind

care.soil_types: Soil with loose texture or soil with specific nutrients
care.fertilizer_type: Dilute fertilizers following instructions, apply 1-2 times monthly
care.light_guide: NC State: Full sun (6 or more hours of direct sunlight a day)
care.tips: Remove aged tissues, dead, rotten, diseased leaves timely | Similar plants: Bignonia capreolata
common_problems: No serious insect or disease problems, however, the species is an aggressive spreader. Trumpet vine is also commonly known as cow-itch vine because some people experience skin redness and itching after coming in contact with the leaves.
common_pests: No serious insect or disease problems, however, the species is an aggressive spreader.

description: After flowering, seeds appear in long narrow pods (to 15 inches long). Pods mature to dark brown in fall and then split open lengthwise to release the seeds. Seed pods give rise to the common name of cigar tree. Seeds ripen from October to December.

care.soil_types: Soil with loose texture or soil with specific nutrients
care.fertilizer_type: Dilute fertilizers following instructions, apply 1-2 times monthly
care.light_guide: NC State: Full sun (6 or more hours of direct sunlight a day)
care.tips: Remove aged tissues, dead and pest branches timely | Similar plants: Catalpa ovata
common_problems: No serious problems. Catalpa sphinx moth caterpillars can do significant damage but are rarely lethal. Mildew, twig blight, verticillium wilt, and leaf spots are occasional problems. Leaves can become damaged and look unsightly from wind or hail.
common_pests: Catalpa sphinx moth caterpillars can do significant damage but are rarely lethal.

description: Bean like fruit up to 22  long dark brown when mature in the fall.  They split lengthwise to release wind-dispersed seeds.  Resemble cigars hence the common name.

care.soil_types: Soil with loose texture or soil with specific nutrients
care.fertilizer_type: Dilute fertilizers following instructions, apply 1-2 times monthly
care.watering_guide: Missouri BG: Easily grown in average, medium to wet, well-drained soils in full sun to part shade. Tolerant of a wide range of soil conditions including both wet and dry soils. Tolerant of seasonal flooding. Prefers moist fertile loams.
care.light_guide: NC State: Full sun (6 or more hours of direct sunlight a day)
care.tips: Remove aged tissues, dead and pest branches timely | Similar plants: Acer saccharinum
common_problems: This species does not have a serious problem with diseases or insects, however, it could be susceptible to verticillium wilt, leaf spot, mildew, and twig blight. The larvae of the catalpa sphinx moth also feed on the foliage, which can result in considerable damage, albeit temporary and non-lethal.
common_pests: This species does not have a serious problem with diseases or insects, however, it could be susceptible to verticillium wilt, leaf spot, mildew, and twig blight.

description: 1/4  wide seed pods that are 6  to 12  long. Winged seeds.

care.light_guide: NC State: Full sun (6 or more hours of direct sunlight a day)
common_problems: No major pest or disease problems

Uses

Does best in hot, dry areas of the garden
Suitable as an accent plant in rock gardens, pollinator gardens, desert gardens, and xeriscaping projects
